course_description:
Analysis of cultural heritage in legislative, practical and ethical frameworks. Cultural aspects of environmental management, including site management, fire management, traditional use of parks and natural resources, and interpretation planning. Review of cultural landscapes concept within a broad conservation framework.

This course examines the principles of cultural heritage management in today's world.ﾠIssues of stakeholder engagement in heritage management and the role of legislation and policy are discussed.

You will learn the practical steps involved in the preparation of a heritage impact assessment. Case studies of cultural heritage management are provided. The relationships between local, state, national and international heritage (especially World Heritage) are reviewed.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims:
The principal aim of the course is to ensure that students understand the place of cultural heritage management in planning, environmental management and planning, and protected area management. To achieve this aim, the course has a number of related subsidiary aims that will assist students to:

1. develop an understanding of the concept of cultural heritage

2. understand new directions in cultural heritage management as they relate to planning and environmental management.

3. understand significance assessment and what is involved in the development of a cultural management plan.

4. understand what is involved in preparing a heritage management plans for cultural heritage places.

learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate an understanding of the concept of cultural heritage
discuss new directions in cultural heritage management
demonstrate an understanding of the basic steps required in planning for cultural heritage management
demonstrate an understanding of the basic steps in preparing heritage management plans and impact statements for cultural heritage places
Develop team building and teamwork skills during Group Work activities

assessment_details.title: Fieldtrip Reflection
assessment_details.task_description: You are required to critically reflect on your visit to Cultural Heritage Places visited on the fieldtrip. Following the fieldtrip, you will write a structured reflection and submit your work before the advised due date and time. The field trip is compulsory and costs approximately $30 per student . It is crictical that students attend the fieldtrip for the best outcome of your assessment. The fieldtrip details will be announced during the semester. Please refer to the Blackboard announcement for the actual cost of the excursion, meeting location, date and time. You will need to organise your own transport to the meeting location at the Museum of Brisbane. Students who miss the field trip will be offered an alternative option to visit the museum independently to still submit the assessment by the due date. The disadvantage is that you will not receive the guided tour experience organised by the Museum. The Course Coordinator will provide the necessary information for students to complete a self‑guided tour and identify the sites they need to visit. You may only be eligible for alternative arrangement if exceptional circumstances arise that prevent you from attending the fieldtrip. Please contact your course coordinator with supporting documentation of the exceptional circumstances that prevented you from attending for consideration of an alternate arrangement. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
You may be granted an extension on the submission of the Reflection only. An approved extension is not applicable to the field trip requirement. You may only be eligible for alternative arrangement if exceptional circumstances arise that prevent you from attending the field trip. Please contact your course coordinator with supporting documentation of the exceptional circumstances that prevented you from attending for consideration of an alternate arrangement.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (the assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Heritage Impact Assessment (Group Report)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: You will form groups of 4 to 5 students to create a heritage impact assessment report for a proposed development in Brisbane. In addition, you will be asked to assess the contribution made by your fellow group members to the assessment task using a peer assessment tool. Those students who do not make a satisfactory contribution to the work may be marked down. Further details of this assessment will be made available on Blackboard. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Heritage Impact Assessment (Group Presentation)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In addition to the Heritage Impact Assessment Report, your group will give a 10 minute PowerPoint in-person summarising the findings of your assessment. Your group will be scheduled to have an in-class presentation with the Course Coordinator between 12.00 pm and 15.00 pm This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
